target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
# Human Intent
Users should be able to use supported upstream OpenClaw runtime plugins with nix-openclaw the same way they use them in regular OpenClaw, but declaratively and reproducibly.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Maintainer Decisions
- User config stays simple: `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "slack" "whatsapp" "copilot" ];`.
- `runtimePlugins` accepts supported OpenClaw plugin ids, not raw upstream install strings.
- `npm:`, `clawhub:`, git, local path, archive, and marketplace install specs are not Home Manager inputs in this PR.
- Lock/update tooling resolves supported npm and ClawHub sources ahead of time into fixed Nix inputs, then Home Manager loads immutable Nix store plugin roots.
- Direct arbitrary plugin sources remain future work because users would need a real Nix package definition: plugin id, source hash, and dependency hash.
- nix-openclaw tool plugins remain separate from OpenClaw runtime plugins.

# Changes
- Adds shrinkwrap-backed npm dependency materialization for OpenClaw runtime plugin packages.
- Resolves selected ClawHub runtime plugin artifacts to fixed package tarballs and builds them through the same immutable runtime plugin builder path.
- Hardens bundled dependency validation so bundled mode requires declared runtime dependency roots to exist in `node_modules`.
- Regenerates the OpenClaw v2026.6.1 runtime plugin report: 34 supported rows, 4 skipped rows.
- Rewrites the README runtime plugin docs from an end-user point of view:
  - Slack as the channel plugin example.
  - GitHub Copilot as the agent runtime/provider example.
  - Tables comparing upstream `openclaw plugins install ...` commands with nix-openclaw config.
  - Clear failure behavior for unsupported ids and raw install specs.
  - No user-facing “catalog plugin” framing.
- Updates runtime-plugin option/error wording and the matching check assertion to avoid exposing maintainer-only wording to users.

# User-Facing Support Contract
Users select supported ids. They do not choose whether an id came from npm or ClawHub.

Examples in this lock include:
- `slack`, `discord`: channel plugins from npm artifacts with bundled runtime dependencies.
- `whatsapp`, `matrix`: channel plugins resolved through ClawHub, then built as fixed package artifacts.
- `codex`, `acpx`, `copilot`, `memory-lancedb`, `tlon`: shrinkwrapped npm runtime plugins.

Current known packageability gaps are Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom. Their published packages declare runtime dependencies but do not include `npm-shrinkwrap.json` or bundled `node_modules`, so upstream needs to republish them with reproducible dependency material before nix-openclaw can add supported ids for them. The remaining PixVerse skipped row is a duplicate row, not a missing supported plugin.

# Proof
Latest head `8b470a941`:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.config-validity` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-plugin-locks` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci` passed.

Earlier implementation proof on this PR:
- `node --check nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs` passed.
- `node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs` passed.
- `./nix/scripts/update-openclaw-runtime-plugin-locks.mjs --check` passed: 34 supported, 4 skipped.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-brave` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-slack` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-memory-lancedb` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-whatsapp` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-acpx` passed.
- `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#openclaw-runtime-plugin-codex` passed.
- `nix flake show --accept-flake-config` passed.
- `scripts/check-flake-lock-owners.sh` passed.

# Local Review
- End-user docs review found the README was still too internal and pushed for direct upstream links, fewer examples, and less package mechanics. Fixed.
- Source-accuracy review found the Nix-mode warning needed to cover config mutators like `openclaw config set` and `openclaw config patch`, not only plugin install/update/uninstall. Fixed.
- Main review pass removed user-facing “catalog” wording from README and relevant option/error text.

# Merge Notes
- This PR intentionally expands the runtime plugin package support surface. Maintainers should review it as a package contract change.
- The updater is heavier for maintainers because it resolves plugin artifacts and dependency hashes at lock-update time. User activation does not do that work.
- PR #100 overlaps at the broader OpenClaw shrinkwrap layer; final merge order still needs maintainer judgment and fresh final-head CI/Garnix proof.
target.author: joshp123
